title: Unit 03 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Goal Setting and Career Planning.

questions.prompt: What does the S in SMART goals stand for?

choices.text: Specific
choices.feedback: Correct. The S in SMART stands for specific.

choices.text: Simple
choices.feedback: SMART goals should be clear, but the S stands for specific.

choices.text: Scheduled
choices.feedback: A timeline matters, but the S does not stand for scheduled.

choices.text: Strategic
choices.feedback: SMART goals may be strategic, but the S stands for specific.

questions.prompt: A long-term goal usually takes more time to complete than a short-term goal.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Long-term goals typically take more time than short-term goals.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Long-term goals usually take more time to complete.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows academic planning?

choices.text: Choosing classes that support a future career goal
choices.feedback: This is the right idea, but the strongest answer in this set is the one that more clearly emphasizes connection between school choices and future preparation.

choices.text: Ignoring course choices until graduation
choices.feedback: Academic planning requires active decision-making, not ignoring choices.

choices.text: Making school choices that support future goals
choices.feedback: Correct. Academic planning means making school choices that connect to future goals.

choices.text: Choosing activities with no connection to goals
choices.feedback: Planning should connect current actions to future goals.

questions.prompt: A student wants to improve attendance, finish assignments on time, and raise grades by the end of the quarter. What idea does this best connect to?

choices.text: A barrier with no possible solution
choices.feedback: This situation is more connected to planning a goal than to giving up on a barrier.

choices.text: A random decision with no structure
choices.feedback: The example includes structure and direction.

choices.text: A plan that avoids measurement
choices.feedback: The example includes goals that can be checked and measured.

choices.text: A SMART goal with clear actions and a deadline
choices.feedback: Correct. This example includes clear actions and a time frame, which connect to SMART goals.

questions.prompt: A timeline can help a student organize steps in the order they should happen.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. A timeline helps students organize steps in a clear sequence.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Timelines are useful because they show the order of steps.

questions.prompt: What is a barrier in a career or school plan?

choices.text: A step that always guarantees success
choices.feedback: A barrier is a challenge, not a guarantee of success.

choices.text: A challenge that can make progress more difficult
choices.feedback: Correct. A barrier is something that makes progress harder.

choices.text: A reward for completing a goal
choices.feedback: A reward is not the same as a barrier.

choices.text: A final job offer
choices.feedback: A job offer is not a barrier.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes adaptability in planning?

choices.text: Changing a plan when needed so progress can continue
choices.feedback: This is the right idea, but the strongest answer in this set is the one that more directly emphasizes responding to change.

choices.text: Ignoring all problems and hoping they disappear
choices.feedback: Adaptability means responding to challenges, not ignoring them.

choices.text: The ability to adjust when plans or conditions change
choices.feedback: Correct. Adaptability means adjusting when plans or conditions change.

choices.text: Refusing to change a goal under any condition
choices.feedback: Adaptability requires flexibility.

questions.prompt: Which action best supports a long-term career goal?

choices.text: Ignoring current school choices
choices.feedback: Current school choices can support long-term goals.

choices.text: Avoiding all planning until senior year
choices.feedback: Planning earlier usually helps students stay prepared.

choices.text: Choosing current steps that connect to a future goal
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer in this set is the one that most clearly highlights purposeful planning.

choices.text: Taking action now that supports future preparation
choices.feedback: Correct. Long-term goals are supported by useful actions taken in the present.
